title: [Bug]: `--userns nomap` & `keep-id` block `--userns auto`
body:
### Issue Description

If a container exists, that was created with `--userns nomap` or `keep-id` any attempt to create a container with the option `--userns auto` fails with: `Error: creating container storage: could not find enough available IDs`.
When the nomap/keep-id container is removed, creating a container with `--userns auto` works as expected.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. `podman create --userns nomap alpine` - Create container with `--userns nomap` or `keep-id`
2. `podman create --userns auto alpine` - Try to create one with `--userns auto`

### Describe the results you received

Creation of the `--userns auto` Container fails if a container with `--userns nomap` or `--userns keep-id` exists.
`Error: creating container storage: could not find enough available IDs`

### Describe the results you expected

Successful creation of the container in a unique userns.

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

Also tested on podman-4.4.0-rc2

comments.body: it is working as expected.  `userns=auto` uses distinct IDs that are not shared with other pods/containers.  With `--userns=nomap` and `--userns=keep-id` you are already using all the available IDs so there is nothing left for the new container

Oh okay, so nomap and keep-id are passing all available IDs to the container? In my case all 2.147.383.648?
I did not realize that.
I found [documentation](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/options/userns.container.html) mentioning this for keep-id but not for nomap.
`nomap` passes all the available IDs **except** the current user.
I see, thank you for the clarification.

I wrote a blog on this recently.
https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/rootless-podman-user-namespace-modes

Thanks for the blog!
This issue got me thinking about whether it would be desirable/practical to have an optional `size` parameter for `nomap` and `keep-id` like we have for `auto`. Like this: `--userns=nomap:size=1024`.
WDYT
I would be fine with that,  The differnce is userns=auto defaults to size of image, where nomap and keep-id default to all UIDs in /etc/subuid.
